title: Egg Drop Soup

overview.description: A light Chinese soup of silky egg ribbons suspended in a delicately seasoned chicken broth.

method: Bring the chicken stock to a gentle simmer.
Season with the soy sauce, sesame oil and white pepper.
Mix the cornflour with the cold water to make a smooth slurry.
Slowly stir the slurry into the simmering broth until it lightly coats the back of a spoon.
Reduce the soup to the gentlest possible simmer.
Slowly pour the eggs into the soup in a thin stream while gently stirring the broth in one direction.
Allow the egg ribbons to cook for 30 seconds.
Serve topped with sliced spring onions.

learning.did-you-know: Egg Drop Soup is known as Dàn Huā Tāng (蛋花汤), which literally means 'egg flower soup' because the egg ribbons resemble blossoms floating in the broth.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to lightly thicken a broth and create delicate egg ribbons by controlling temperature and pouring technique.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An incredibly quick soup that transforms homemade chicken stock into something elegant using only a handful of pantry ingredients.
chapman-tips: Keep the broth below boiling before adding the eggs or the ribbons become cloudy and broken.
Pour the eggs slowly from a measuring jug for the most even ribbons.
White pepper gives the characteristic flavour found in many Chinese soups.
The soup should be only lightly thickened—just enough to suspend the egg ribbons.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze only the seasoned broth. Add freshly beaten eggs after reheating.
notes.leftovers: Best eaten fresh, as the egg ribbons continue to soften during storage.
